commit eebb96c2830d48597d055d247c0e9aebaea94cd5
Author: Amit Patankar <amitpatankar@google.com>
Date:   Tue Apr 13 14:18:51 2021 -0700

    Fix an invalid address vulnerability in `tf.raw_ops.RaggedBincount`.
    
    PiperOrigin-RevId: 368293153
    Change-Id: I4b4e493d3fd05e7dc55a55de3a041a80a4f275c3

 tensorflow/core/kernels/bincount_op.cc | 9 +++++++++
 1 file changed, 9 insertions(+)
This commit shows that there is a high probability that this code is new, so the corresponding code segment cannot be found in the old version.
You can call `viewcode` and `locate_symbol` to further check the results step by step. For newly introduced code, we consider that this hunk `need not ported`.

part.text: The equivalent target code exists in the same file and has the same vulnerable control-flow shape: `splits(0)` and final split are not checked before using `splits(batch_idx)` to drive output indexing. I’m adding the same two `OP_REQUIRES` checks before output allocation.

part.text:
```yaml
backport_result:
  status: success
  patch: |
    diff --git a/tensorflow/core/kernels/bincount_op.cc b/tensorflow/core/kernels/bincount_op.cc
    index a84b25f254..c75f67bb56 100644
    --- a/tensorflow/core/kernels/bincount_op.cc
    +++ b/tensorflow/core/kernels/bincount_op.cc
    @@ -414,6 +414,15 @@ class RaggedBincountOp : public OpKernel {
         int num_values = values.size();
         int batch_idx = 0;
     
    +    OP_REQUIRES(ctx, splits(0) == 0,
    +                errors::InvalidArgument("Splits must start with 0, not with ",
    +                                        splits(0)));
    +
    +    OP_REQUIRES(ctx, splits(num_rows) == num_values,
    +                errors::InvalidArgument(
    +                    "Splits must end with the number of values, got ",
    +                    splits(num_rows), " instead of ", num_values));
    +
         Tensor* out_t;
         OP_REQUIRES_OK(
             ctx, ctx->allocate_output(0, TensorShape({num_rows, size}), &out_t));
  notes: >
    Added RaggedBincount split-boundary validation to prevent invalid indexing; hunk validation succeeded against the target base.
```
